UIDictionary.Quantum_Rocks_Popup_Message:
Quantum Rocks：
It is recommended to play this mod after completing the base game content.
Chat with Slate to begin. Have fun!
UIDictionary.Quantum_Rocks_GameOverText:
You successfully reached the Eye of the Universe via the Quantum Moon.
They welcomes you.

DialogueDictionary.Answer_Slate_1: I discovered that quantum rocks are actually a form of intelligent life!
DialogueDictionary.Answer_Slate_2: And the Quantum Moon is their spaceship!
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Answer_1_1: Are you serious? You mean these rocks can actually think, talk, and have been watching us this whole time, but only deign to communicate with you?
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Answer_1_2: Look, I know the first launch is stressful, but you might be overreacting a bit.
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Answer_2_2: I should focus on fixing the autopilot's evasion function instead.
DialogueDictionary.Not_Noob: This isn't my first launch anymore.
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Answer_4_1: This must be related to that 'time loop' you keep talking about, right?
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Answer_4_2: Honestly, I have no idea what you're talking about.
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Quantum_1: As a matter of fact, I heard that quantum shard in the Observatory is acting strange.
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Quantum_2: Hornfels says it makes weird noises at night. Though I think he's just hallucinating from overwork.
DialogueDictionary.Slate_Hint: Hey! Could you check out that quantum shard in the Observatory for me?
DialogueDictionary.Ok_Move_On: Alright, I'll keep an eye out for it.
DialogueDictionary.Keep_Keep_Staring: *Keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps, keeps staring at it*
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Yield: ......<![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>Alright, alright, stop staring at me. I give up.
DialogueDictionary.Oh_My: Oh My Hearth you can talk!
DialogueDictionary.Oh_My_Shard: Oh My Shard you can talk! <![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>Just kidding. I've been observing you all for a long time.
DialogueDictionary.Time_Loop_1: I know who you are. I spoke with you in the last time loop.
DialogueDictionary.Time_Loop_2: A time loop? So the Nomai's plan actually succeeded......
DialogueDictionary.Time_Loop_3: I thought you already knew, through your......telepathy.
DialogueDictionary.Time_Loop_4: No, we can only perceive the dominant thoughts of others—more like talking across space, really. I don't think 'telepathy' is the right word, but we can't physically speak, and it's the closest term I could find in your language.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard: I'll bet you have plenty of questions. Ask away.
DialogueDictionary.Who_Are_You: Who are you? Why can you talk?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_1: We call ourselves......<![CDATA[<Pause=1>]]>Sdrahs Mutnauq. But you may refer to us however you like.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_2: My name isOrange]]>.]]>......No, wait, it'sOrange]]>. I don't actually speak, by the way—I project words directly to you using what you'd calltelepathy]].
DialogueDictionary.Other_Planet_Shard: We? Can other quantum shards communicate too?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_1_1: Of course. They're scattered across nearly every planet—you should go talk to them.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_1_2: They might be hesitant to talk at first, but just mention my name and they'll understand.
DialogueDictionary.How_Telepathy: How do you do that telepathy thing?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_2_1: How do you talk? I mean, your ability to speak is just asunusual]]> to us.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_1_2_2: We just......do telepathy. It's like how you just know how to speak.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_2_1: Honestly, I don't know. I just existed, and suddenly I was conscious.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_2_2: From what I've observed, your ancestors came to be much the same way, didn't they? Who can say how it happened—one moment they were inorganic matter, the next organic, then alive, then intelligent. Fascinating, isn't it?
DialogueDictionary.Observe_Hearthian: What do you mean by 'observing us for a long time'?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_3_1: Yes, we arrived in this star system long ago—even before the Nomai. We've been watching ever since.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_3_2: I saw green solar sails vanish without a trace, watched Nomai expeditions study this planet, and saw the first Hearthian ship launch into the void.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Talk: Why are you talking to me now?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_4_1: Mainly because quantum objects are a vital part of this story too. Why does everyone only care about the Nomai and Hearthians? We're just as fascinating, and we have plenty of stories to tell.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_4_2: Secondly, I think the time is right for you to meet us.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_4_3: And thirdly, someone thought making a mod where you talk to quantum rocks was a good idea.
DialogueDictionary.Answer_Shard_4: Uh......I have no idea what you're talking about.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Stay_Here: Why did you choose to stay here?
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_5_1: Actually, I was moved here by......Hornfels, I think?......I was quite surprised at first.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_5_2: I grew to like you all quickly, so I even altered my quantum collapse position to stay.
DialogueDictionary.Shard_Answer_6: Hey, buddy. We're not far enough apart that we need to send messages like that—we can still telepathize!
DialogueDictionary.Answer_Shard_6: Alright then. Tell him I said hi back.
DialogueDictionary.No_More_Question: I don't have any more questions for now.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Orange: <![CDATA[<size=25>The <color=orange>Orange</color> one from the Observatory?</size>]]><![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>Alright, I guess you're telling the truth.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Introduction: Greetings. I am<![CDATA[<color=green>Green</color>]]>. I'd shake your hand, but I don't have one.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Timber_Shard: I know you want to talk—go ahead.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Answer_1_1: <![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> is our leader—all quantum shards know him.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Answer_1_2: Besides that, he's my best friend.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Stay_Timber: Why did you choose to stay on Timber Hearth?
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Answer_2: I love the streams and meadows, and the sound of wind through the treetops.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Answer_3_1: I was born with them. I used to think everything had<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> until I came here.
DialogueDictionary.Timber_Shard_Answer_3_2: I think objects near me gainquantum properties]]> after being around me long enough. Want to test it? It only takes about22 minutes]].
DialogueDictionary.I_Know_You_1: Stop, don't speak yet.<![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>I know why you're here.
DialogueDictionary.I_Know_You_2: Forgive my directness, but you don't seem to like rising sand in confined spaces, so I'll make this quick.
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Introduction: You may call me<![CDATA[<color=yellow>Yellow</color>]]>. It's a pleasure to meet you too.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Stay_Hourglass: Why did you choose to stay on the Hourglass Twins?
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_1_1: I love small spaces—they give me a sense of security. Besides, I enjoy the embrace of the sand.
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_1_2: The sand seems to like me a lot!
DialogueDictionary.Answer_Hourglass_Shard_1: They don't seem to like me very much, though.
DialogueDictionary.What_If_Sand_Block: What happens if your quantum position gets buried by sand?
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_2_1: It's no trouble for me—the sand makes way for me.
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_2_2: And if they don't, I can just push them aside!
DialogueDictionary.Why_Skeleton_Quantum: Why do those......Nomai skeletons have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> too? And sometimes they stand up!
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_3_1: Probably because they're curious.<![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>Between us, there's still a flicker of consciousness left in those skeletons......Let me check......
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_3_2: The Nomai skeletons send you their greetings, hatchling! They're delighted to see other intelligent life exploring the Nomai's and quantum objects' mysteries.
DialogueDictionary.Answer_Hourglass_Shard_3: Thank you.<![CDATA[<size=25>That's honestly a bit creepy.</size>]]>
DialogueDictionary.Know_Quantum_Rule: Did you know the Nomai discovered a new quantum rule thanks to you?
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_4_1: I do. They even found my secret hiding spot in the process.
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Answer_4_2: If you ask me, that quantum rule is a bit odd. You have other sensory organs besides your eyes, after all.
DialogueDictionary.Hourglass_Bye: Farewell! If you need to move quickly through the caves, feel free to come find me!
DialogueDictionary.Try_Talk: Hello,<![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> said I could talk to you.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Introduction: My friends call me Black. Welcome!
DialogueDictionary.Why_Stay_Brittle: Why did you choose to stay on Brittle Hollow?
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_1_1: I find it quite......peaceful here. Brittle Hollow's colors and atmosphere are quite charming, not to mention the little black hole beneath us.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_1_2: Though meteorites have been falling from the sky lately. I have a bad feeling about it.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Trees_Quantum: Why do these trees have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>?
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_2_1: Probably because they're tired of staying in the same place forever.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_2_2: I don't know for sure, though. I can't sense their minds—if they even have any.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_3_1: Mm-hmm. The Nomai weren't very gentle when they installed these things.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_3_2: Still, I'm glad they noticed me and added these nice decorations to help others notice and understand us. If there was a beauty contest, I'd undoubtedly win first prize.
DialogueDictionary.Know_Tower: Do you know about the Tower of Quantum Knowledge the Nomai built?
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_4_1: I do. I watched the Nomai build it and use it from start to finish.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_4_2: They were brilliant to figure out how to locate the Quantum Moon.
DialogueDictionary.What_Quantum_Moon: Oh right, what is the Quantum Moon? How did it end up here?
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_1: Unfortunately, we don't have the answer to that either.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_2: All we know is that the earliest memory of every quantum shard is of the Quantum Moon—specifically, the Quantum Moon in its Eye of the Universe form. We were all once rocks on the Quantum Moon.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_3: Based on the Quantum Moon's material and properties, we suspect it might have once been part of the Eye of the Universe.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_4: Then one day,<![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> noticed the Quantum Moon had taken on several different forms.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_5: We soon learned these forms were because the Quantum Moon had become a satellite of several new planets. The reason for this is still a mystery to us.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_6: Does the Quantum Moon—or even the Eye of the Universe—have consciousness? So far, all our attempts to communicate have gone unanswered.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_7: If you want to explore the Quantum Moon, by all means—just make sure you have the relevant knowledge first.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Answer_5_8: I recommend talking to the other quantum shards before visiting the Quantum Moon.
DialogueDictionary.Brittle_Bye: Farewell! Don't fall into the black hole!
DialogueDictionary.Meet_Giants_Shard_1: Wonderful! Another visitor at last!<![CDATA[<size=25>Wait, I wasn't supposed to say that out loud......</size>]]>
DialogueDictionary.Meet_Giants_Shard_3: Oh, good. My apologies, I'm just overexcited. It's been a long time since any new intelligent life has visited here.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Giants_Shard: Welcome to the Tower of Quantum Trials!
DialogueDictionary.Why_Stay_Giants: Why did you choose to stay on Giant's Deep? More specifically, in this tower?
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_1_1: I initially chose to stay on the other islands, enjoying the rain, waves, and tornadoes.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_1_2: But when the Nomai arrived, I discovered they were building a tower to teach and test their quantum knowledge.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_1_3: I was deeply honored, so I decided to act as an observer and examiner here once the tower was complete.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_1_4: The day the Tower of Quantum Trials was finished was the happiest day of my existence.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Tower_Quantum: Why do specific things in this tower have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>?
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_2_1: I gave the Nomai a little help while they were building the tower.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_2_2: They were quite surprised—came up with all sorts of hypotheses and theories.
DialogueDictionary.Why_Quantum_Signal: Why do you emit quantum signals?
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_3_1: It's just an innate trait of mine. Let me think......<![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>It's like how you emit heat into your surroundings.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_3_2: Though my signals travel much farther.
DialogueDictionary.How_Get_Here: How did you all get here?
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_4_1: We came here aboard theQuantum Moon]]. Didn't the other shards tell you?
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Shard_Answer_4_2: If not, go find Black. He knows the most about the Quantum Moon.
DialogueDictionary.Giants_Bye: Farewell! I believe we'll meet again soon.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_Hi: Greetings, hatchling. It's good to see you again! It seems you've learned quite a bit about us.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_0: Welcome to our humble home. It has been our home for a very long time—at least.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_Answer_1_2: I just choose to collapse to a few fixed locations when observed—it makes me feel more at ease.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_1_2: There's an unauthorised structure behind me.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_Answer_2_1: You mean the Quantum Shrine the Nomai built? I originally wanted to protest their construction, but I later realized the structure actually helps other intelligent life visit.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_Answer_2_2: Besides that, for some reason their altar has<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> too—most likely influenced by the Quantum Moon. Perhaps that means the Quantum Moon approved of the project, so I have no objections either.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_1_3: There's a Nomai at the South Pole who's somehow both alive and dead.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1_Answer_3_1: We'll talk about that when we get there. Though I'm already there......You know what I mean.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Hi: Aha. See that vortex above us? It only appears when the Quantum Moon is in its Eye of the Universe form, so we suspect this vortex is a special projection of the Eye of the Universe on the Quantum Moon.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_0: We've always wanted to go inside—we have no quantum position on the Eye of the Universe, nor can we collapse there. But we......<![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>can't fly.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_2: Would you be willing to go inside for us?
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_2_1: First, I want to ask about Solanum, the Nomai.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_1_1: Honestly, I'm just as surprised as you are. Does the Quantum Moon have such power? I've never heard of anything like it, have you?
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_1_2: She's not just in a superposition of life and death—time has lost all meaning for her too.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_1_3: Though that's true for us quantum shards as well......
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_2_2: Wait, you've never been to the Eye of the Universe?
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_2_1: No. Before the Quantum Moon became a satellite of other planets, we didn't even know we could leave this place to go elsewhere.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_2_2: All we know about the Eye of the Universe is its general appearance, that it emits quantum signals, and that it's what the Quantum Moon orbits.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_4_1: Oh. "Why is there an Eye of the Universe? Why is there a Quantum Moon? How doquantum properties]]> even exist? Ifquantum properties]]>originate from the Eye of the Universe]], why do other things—related or not—havequantum properties]]> too? Why do things withquantum properties]]> only collapse whenobserved by intelligent beings]]>? Why do they only collapse to a few specific locations instead of anywhere else? Why does only the Quantum Moon collapse into vastly different forms? Which of these forms is the Quantum Moon'strue form]]>? What did the Quantum Moon look like before it hadquantum properties]]>? Why doestime lose meaning]]> on the Eye of the Universe and Quantum Moon? Why is Solanum only partially alive?What would happen if she left the Quantum Moon? Why is Solanum—an intelligent being—present in all states of the Quantum Moon at once, while I only exist in one? How does consciousness and intelligence emerge from nothing? How did time, dimensions, and matter emerge from nothing? Is the time loop a series of parallel worlds, or the same world repeating?]]>"
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_4_2: "What does it mean to 'break the fabric of spacetime'? Has no one ever broken the fabric of spacetime before me?What's really going on with Dark Bramble?How did the universe come to be? Why do we exist in a three-dimensional world?Do we even really exist, or is all of this justa simulation or a figment of imagination!?]]>"
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_5: Hey! Hatchling, look at me—calm down!
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_6: <![CDATA[<Pause/>]]>You really need to rest. Return to Timber Hearth, stare at the campfire under the stars until you fall asleep. Don't think about all this.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_7: After all, if every question had an answer, there would be no need for exploration and discovery. And wouldn't that be boring?
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_3_8: At least we still have the chance to explore. At least you do.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Observatory_Shard_2_4: I think I'll wait a bit first.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_5_1: Good luck. I always like to say that—luck is one of the few things we can't control, yet it's crucial.
DialogueDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2_Answer_5_2: Farewell. I hope we meet again.
DialogueDictionary.Ernesto_Hi_1: Hey it's me Ernesto the magic talking quantum anglerfish.
DialogueDictionary.Ernesto_Hi_2: Looks like it's a cloudy day today.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Ernesto_1: Why do you have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> too?
DialogueDictionary.Ernesto_Answer_1_1: Of course I do. How else do you think I exist in so many places at once?
DialogueDictionary.Ernesto_Answer_1_2: Have you ever seen the true image of something outside your camera's view? When you're not looking, it disappears.]]> Actually, everything hasquantum properties]]>—most things just only have one quantum collapse position, that's all.]]>
DialogueDictionary.Ernesto_Answer_2: It's a side effect of having<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>, I suppose.
DialogueDictionary.Ask_Ernesto_3: Say hi to Geswaldo for me.

ShipLogDictionary.Observatory_Shard_Rumor: That quantum shard in the Observatory seems to be acting odd.
ShipLogDictionary.Observatory_Shard_1: The quantum shard in the Observatory spoke to me! It said its name is<![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]>, and it has been observing this place with other quantum shards all along.
ShipLogDictionary.Observatory_Shard_2: <![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> suggested I go talk to the other quantum shards.
ShipLogDictionary.Timber_Shard_Rumor: Besides the quantum shard in the Observatory, there seems to be another shard on Timber Hearth.
ShipLogDictionary.Timber_Shard_1: The quantum shard in the Quantum Grove is named<![CDATA[<color=green>Green</color>]]>, and he's friends with<![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Timber_Shard_2: <![CDATA[<color=green>Green</color>]]> believes other things gain<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> from being near him for too long.
ShipLogDictionary.Hourglass_Quantum_Shard: Quantum Shard on the Hourglass Twins
ShipLogDictionary.Hourglass_Quantum_Shard?: Quantum Shard on the Hourglass Twins?
ShipLogDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_Rumor: There appears to be another quantum shard on the Hourglass Twins.
ShipLogDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_1: The quantum shard in the Quantum Cave is named<![CDATA[<color=yellow>Yellow</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Hourglass_Shard_2: <![CDATA[<color=yellow>Yellow</color>]]> believes other things have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> because they still have consciousness and subjectively want to possess<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Brittle_Shard_Rumor: There seems to be another quantum shard on Brittle Hollow.
ShipLogDictionary.Brittle_Shard_1: The quantum shard on the Tower of Quantum Knowledge is named Black.
ShipLogDictionary.Brittle_Shard_2: Black believes other things have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]> because they don't want to stay in the same place.
ShipLogDictionary.Brittle_Shard_3: Black told me what he knows about the Quantum Moon, and advised me to talk to the other quantum shards before visiting it.
ShipLogDictionary.Giants_Shard_Rumor: There appears to be another quantum shard on Giant's Deep.
ShipLogDictionary.Giants_Shard_1: The quantum shard in the Tower of Quantum Trials is named<![CDATA[<color=blue>Blue</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Giants_Shard_2: <![CDATA[<color=blue>Blue</color>]]> claims he's the reason other things have<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_Spaceship?: Quantum Moon (Eye of the Universe Form)?
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_Rumor: So far, all I know is that the quantum shards originated from the Quantum Moon's Eye of the Universe form, and they came here aboard the Quantum Moon.
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_1:
It's said the quantum shards originated from the Quantum Moon's Eye of the Universe form, and they came here aboard the Quantum Moon.
<![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> greeted me on the Quantum Moon in its Eye of the Universe form.
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_2: For some reason, the Quantum Shrine built by the Nomai also has<![CDATA[<color=orange>quantum properties</color>]]>.
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_3: <![CDATA[<color=orange>Orange</color>]]> wants me to enter the vortex—the projection of the Eye of the Universe on the Quantum Moon.
ShipLogDictionary.Quantum_Moon_4: The quantum shards don't understand what's happening to Solanum either.
